meta.description: Three targeted changes to Header block: focus-visible accent color, outline fix, dropdown min-width fix

changes.title: Focus-visible accent: apply text-accent color on focus-visible for all interactive elements
changes.description: When an interactive element in the header receives focus-visible, it should show text-accent color (same as hover). Currently, focus-visible only applies outline styles (outline-2 outline-white outline-offset-2) but does NOT change the text color. The change is to add `focus-visible:text-accent` alongside the existing `hover:text-accent` on every interactive element.

Button className for item with submenu

Currently no focus-visible outline — add full focus-visible styling
Currently no focus-visible outline — add full focus-visible styling
Currently no focus-visible outline — add full focus-visible styling

decisions.decision: Add focus-visible:text-accent to all interactive header elements that already have hover:text-accent
decisions.evidence: User explicitly requested: 'When focus-visible, apply text-accent color (same as hover) on all interactive elements in the header'

changes.title: Outline color fix: remove conflicting outline base state, use proper Tailwind v4 approach
changes.description:
INVESTIGATION FINDINGS:

The current approach uses: `outline outline-0 outline-transparent` as a base state, with `focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-white focus-visible:outline-offset-2` for the focus state.

There is a GLOBAL rule in `resources/css/screen/body.css` (line 38-39):
```css
*:focus-visible {
  @apply outline-2 outline-black outline-dotted outline-offset-1;
}
```

This global rule applies `outline-black` and `outline-dotted` to ALL elements on focus-visible. The problem is a SPECIFICITY CONFLICT:

1. In Tailwind v4, `outline` generates `outline-style: solid` (it's the shorthand utility for setting outline-style). `outline-0` generates `outline-width: 0px`. `outline-transparent` generates `outline-color: transparent`.
2. The global `*:focus-visible` rule uses `@apply` which resolves to plain CSS properties. The Tailwind utility classes `focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-white` on the elements SHOULD override the global rule because utility classes have higher specificity than `*:focus-visible` in Tailwind v4's layer ordering.
3. HOWEVER, the global rule sets `outline-dotted` (outline-style: dotted) and the element's focus-visible classes do NOT reset the outline-style — so the element gets `outline-style: dotted` from the global rule instead of `solid`.
4. Additionally, the global rule sets `outline-black` and the competing `outline-white` may have a specificity tie depending on Tailwind's internal ordering.

ROOT CAUSE: The `outline outline-0 outline-transparent` base state is unnecessary and potentially harmful. The real issue is the GLOBAL `*:focus-visible` rule in body.css which applies black dotted outline to everything. The header's `focus-visible:outline-white` fights with the global `focus-visible:outline-black`.

SOLUTION:
1. Remove `outline outline-0 outline-transparent` from the base state of all header elements (it does nothing useful and adds confusion).
2. Add `focus-visible:outline-solid` to explicitly set outline-style on focus-visible (overriding the global dotted).
3. Keep `focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-white focus-visible:outline-offset-2`.
4. The final focus-visible class set becomes: `focus-visible:outline-solid focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-white focus-visible:outline-offset-2`

Alternative (simpler): Since the header is a dark background element, we could also add a scoped override in the header's CSS. But per-element utility classes are more explicit and consistent with the project's approach.

decisions.decision: Remove outline outline-0 outline-transparent base state and add focus-visible:outline-solid to override global *:focus-visible { outline-dotted outline-black } rule
decisions.evidence: Global rule in body.css line 38: *:focus-visible { @apply outline-2 outline-black outline-dotted outline-offset-1; } — sets dotted+black outline on ALL elements. Header elements need to explicitly override with outline-solid and outline-white on focus-visible.

changes.title: Dropdown min-width: recalculate based on actual padding value
changes.description:
INVESTIGATION FINDINGS:

In HeaderMainItem.php (line 55), the submenu dropdown has:
- `md:p-6` (padding: 1.5rem = 24px on all sides)
- `md:-ml-6` (negative left margin to align with parent, offsetting the left padding)
- `md:min-w-[calc(100%+3rem)]` (parent width + 3rem)

The logic: The dropdown should appear at least as wide as its parent trigger. Since the dropdown has padding (p-6 = 1.5rem per side), the content area is smaller than the total width. To make the dropdown's content area at least as wide as the parent, we need: min-width = 100% (parent width) + left padding + right padding = 100% + 1.5rem + 1.5rem = 100% + 3rem.

BUT: The dropdown also has `md:-ml-6` which shifts it left by 1.5rem (the left padding). This means the dropdown's LEFT edge starts 1.5rem to the left of the parent. So the content area's left edge aligns with the parent's left edge. For the content area's RIGHT edge to also align with (or exceed) the parent's right edge, we need:

min-width = 100% + left-margin-offset + right-padding = 100% + 1.5rem + 1.5rem = 100% + 3rem

So `calc(100%+3rem)` IS actually correct for p-6 (1.5rem * 2 = 3rem). The current value matches the formula: parent width + padding*2.

HOWEVER: the user said the current value isn't right. Let me check if maybe the padding was changed and the min-width wasn't updated. The PHP shows `md:p-6` and `md:min-w-[calc(100%+3rem)]`. p-6 = 1.5rem, 1.5rem * 2 = 3rem. These are in sync.

Wait — there's also `md:min-w-max` before `md:min-w-[calc(100%+3rem)]`. In Tailwind v4, when both utilities apply, the later one wins (same specificity, source order). But `min-w-max` sets `min-width: max-content`. Having BOTH is contradictory. The `min-w-max` ensures the dropdown is at least as wide as its content, and `min-w-[calc(100%+3rem)]` ensures it's at least as wide as the parent + padding. CSS `min-width` only takes ONE value, so the LAST one wins in the class list. In Tailwind v4, property conflicts are resolved by the CSS cascade, not class order. Both generate the same property (`min-width`), so whichever has higher specificity or later source order wins.

This is actually the real bug: `md:min-w-max` and `md:min-w-[calc(100%+3rem)]` conflict. Remove `md:min-w-max` and keep the calc.

The padding IS p-6, so calc(100% + 3rem) is mathematically correct. But to make the formula self-documenting and dynamic (if padding ever changes), express it as: `md:min-w-[calc(100%+theme(spacing.6)*2)]` or simply keep `calc(100%+3rem)` since p-6 = 1.5rem and 1.5rem*2 = 3rem.

FINAL DECISION: Remove the conflicting `md:min-w-max`, keep `md:min-w-[calc(100%+3rem)]` which is correct for p-6 padding. Also update the TSX to match.

elements.note: Remove md:min-w-max which conflicts with md:min-w-[calc(100%+3rem)]. The calc value is correct for p-6 (1.5rem * 2 = 3rem).
Remove md:min-w-max which conflicts with md:min-w-[calc(100%+3rem)]. Keep the calc.

decisions.decision: Remove conflicting md:min-w-max from dropdown submenu. Keep md:min-w-[calc(100%+3rem)] which correctly represents parent-width + (p-6 * 2) = 100% + 3rem
decisions.evidence: Dropdown has md:p-6 (1.5rem) and md:-ml-6. Formula: 100% + padding*2 = 100% + 1.5rem*2 = 100% + 3rem. The md:min-w-max utility conflicts because both set the same CSS property (min-width) and Tailwind v4 resolves the conflict unpredictably.

verificationSteps: Tab through all header nav items — each should show accent color text AND white solid outline on focus-visible
Tab into submenu items — same accent color + white solid outline
Tab to search button, SEP link — same treatment
Verify NO black dotted outline appears on any header element
Open a dropdown submenu and verify its width is at least parent width + padding (visually check alignment)
Check mobile view: tab through mobile menu items, verify same focus-visible treatment

decisions.decision: Add focus-visible:text-accent to all interactive header elements
decisions.evidence: User requested accent color on focus-visible, matching hover behavior

decisions.decision: Remove outline outline-0 outline-transparent base; add focus-visible:outline-solid to override global dotted outline
decisions.evidence: Global body.css rule: *:focus-visible { outline-2 outline-black outline-dotted outline-offset-1 } conflicts with header's focus-visible:outline-white. Need explicit outline-solid to override outline-dotted.

decisions.decision: Remove conflicting md:min-w-max from dropdown, keep calc(100%+3rem)
decisions.evidence: Two min-width utilities on same element conflict in Tailwind v4. calc value is correct for p-6 padding.
